verses.1: My son, if you have become collateral for your neighbor,
verses.2: you are trapped by the words of your mouth;
verses.3: Do this now, my son, and deliver yourself,
verses.4: Give no sleep to your eyes,
verses.5: Free yourself, like a gazelle from the hand of the hunter,
verses.6: Go to the ant, you sluggard.
verses.7: which having no chief, overseer, or ruler,
verses.8: provides her bread in the summer,
verses.9: How long will you sleep, sluggard?
verses.10: A little sleep, a little slumber,
verses.11: so your poverty will come as a robber,
verses.12: A worthless person, a man of iniquity,
verses.13: who winks with his eyes, who signals with his feet,
verses.15: Therefore his calamity will come suddenly.
verses.16: There are six things which the LORD hates;
verses.18: a heart that devises wicked schemes,
verses.19: a false witness who utters lies,
verses.20: My son, keep your father’s commandment,
verses.21: Bind them continually on your heart.
verses.22: When you walk, it will lead you.
verses.23: For the commandment is a lamp,
verses.24: to keep you from the immoral woman,
verses.25: Don’t lust after her beauty in your heart,
verses.26: For a prostitute reduces you to a piece of bread.
verses.27: Can a man scoop fire into his lap,
verses.28: Or can one walk on hot coals,
verses.29: So is he who goes in to his neighbor’s wife.
verses.31: but if he is found, he shall restore seven times.
verses.32: He who commits adultery with a woman is void of understanding.
verses.33: He will get wounds and dishonor.
verses.34: For jealousy arouses the fury of the husband.
